method: league.json overall ownership base + GW38 top-10k captaincy share (Haaland ~35%, B.Fernandes ~26%, Bowen ~10%) layered into EO estimates

template_core.note: Owned by user. Highest EO in the game GW38; not captaining = top rank-loss vector

differentials.why: AVL away at MCI caps floor, but sub-8% EO and set-piece/attacking role give a real rank swing on a haul
AVL penalty/big-chance share; genuine differential striker under 8% EO
CHE midfield goal threat, low elite ownership
NEW midfielder with attacking output; true sub-8% differential
ARS defender, clean-sheet + attacking returns, low-owned defensive differential

rank_risks.detail: User C=Bowen (~10% share) vs field/Gemini consensus Haaland (~35%). Largest rank-swing exposure for a rank-7 manager chasing the field in the final GW

rank_risks.risk: Haaland blank while captained by field
rank_risks.detail: User owns him (V is Gabriel), so partially covered, but losing the ~35% captain multiplier vs leaders hurts
Template DEF; neutral vs field since widely owned. Used as vice only

captain_note: User captain = Bowen, a ~10% differential punt. League and Gemini consensus is Haaland (~35%). For a rank-7 manager defending position, captaining Bowen is high-variance — justified only as an aggressive chase, not a defend-rank move.
